title: CLI: apply pad() in MLS-encrypted broadcast send path
description:
What: Wrap the MLS ciphertext with pad() in cli/src/commands.rs before it is placed in BroadcastParams.payload, so every broadcast carries a bucket-aligned payload.

Algorithm:
1. Add to imports: use nie_core::messages::pad;
2. Find all sites in the chat() function where MLS ciphertext is produced and placed into BroadcastParams or SealedBroadcastParams payload. There are approximately three:
   a. Main broadcast path (after mls.encrypt()): let mls_ciphertext = mls.encrypt(&line_bytes)?; -> let payload = pad(&mls_ciphertext)?;
   b. Sealed broadcast path (HPKE path): payload that goes into seal_message() before sealing
   c. Group send path (/group send): after mls.encrypt_for_group()
3. For the non-MLS plaintext path (mls_active == false): do NOT pad — padding interoperates only with unpad() which expects the prefix. Plaintext path is transitional and will be removed when MLS is mandatory.
4. On pad() error (payload > 4092 bytes): eprintln error message, continue (do not crash)

Defensive rules:
- Never pad the plaintext (pre-MLS) path — only MLS-encrypted ciphertext
- On pad() Err: print error to user, continue loop, do not panic or crash
- Do not log the plaintext or ciphertext content

Team: standard R+I+T
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-cli passes; cargo clippy -p nie-cli -- -D warnings passes; grep for pad() usage appears in commands.rs at MLS-active send site
